title: • Twist (Ten): A sudden shift changes the entire perspective.
content:
• Twist (Ten): A sudden shift changes the entire perspective.
• Conclusion (Ketsu): The shift is absorbed, leading to resolution.

Activity: Expand Your Logline Using These Structures
1. Take your best logline and develop it using each of the three structures.
2. Write a brief outline for each version.
3. Identify which structure feels most natural for your story.

Final Takeaways

✅ A great story comes from layering multiple inspirations. Don’t just take from one source—blend elements in a way that feels personal.
✅ Your first drafts are exploratory. Most of what you write at first is for you, not for the final product.
✅ Characters must make choices. A passive protagonist kills momentum.
✅ A story moves through tension and release. Every scene should shift in some way—a change in emotion, stakes, or perception.
✅ Your first challenge is to write a logline and develop it in multiple ways.
context: From document: The Graphic Novel Writing Guide for Derek: Inspiration, Structure… — chunk 7 (chunk 7/22)
